# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: External Contributor PR Testing

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write
  issues: write

jobs:
  security-check:
    name: Security Check
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      approved: ${{ steps.check.outputs.approved }}
    steps:
      - name: Check if PR is from fork
        id: check
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}" != "${{ github.repository }}" ]; then
            echo "PR is from a fork"
            echo "approved=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "PR is from same repository"
            echo "approved=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Check author association
        if: steps.check.outputs.approved == 'false'
        run: |
          ASSOCIATION="${{ github.event.pull_request.author_association }}"
          if [[ "$ASSOCIATION" == "OWNER" ]] || [[ "$ASSOCIATION" == "MEMBER" ]] || [[ "$ASSOCIATION" == "COLLABORATOR" ]]; then
            echo "approved=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Require manual approval
        if: steps.check.outputs.approved == 'false'
        run: |
          echo "::warning::This PR is from an external contributor and requires manual approval"
          echo "Please review the changes and add the 'safe-to-test' label to proceed"
          exit 1

  await-approval:
    name: Await Manual Approval
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: security-check
    if: failure() && needs.security-check.outputs.approved == 'false'
    steps:
      - name: Wait for approval label
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const labels = context.payload.pull_request.labels.map(l => l.name);
            if (!labels.includes('safe-to-test')) {
              core.setFailed('PR requires manual approval. Add "safe-to-test" label to proceed.');
            }

  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: security-check
    if: |
      needs.security-check.outputs.approved == 'true' || 
      contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'safe-to-test')
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout base repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Checkout PR code in isolated directory
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          path: pr-code
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Scan for secrets in PR
        run: |
          cd pr-code
          if grep -r -i -E "(password|secret|api[_-]?key|token|credential)" .; then
            echo "::warning::Potential secrets detected in PR code"
          fi

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18'
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: pr-code/package-lock.json

      - name: Install dependencies with audit
        run: |
          cd pr-code
          npm ci --ignore-scripts
          npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Run linter
        id: lint
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          cd pr-code
          npm run lint 2>&1 | tee ../lint-output.txt

      - name: Run tests in sandboxed environment
        id: test
        continue-on-error: true
        env:
          NODE_ENV: test
          CI: true
        run: |
          cd pr-code
          timeout 600 npm test -- --coverage --maxWorkers=2 2>&1 | tee ../test-output.txt

      - name: Run security scan
        id: security
        continue-on-error: true
        run: |
          cd pr-code
          npm run security-check 2>&1 | tee ../security-output.txt || true

      - name: Generate test report
        if: always()
        id: report
        run: |
          LINT_STATUS="${{ steps.lint.outcome }}"
          TEST_STATUS="${{ steps.test.outcome }}"
          SECURITY_STATUS="${{ steps.security.outcome }}"
          
          echo "LINT_STATUS=$LINT_STATUS" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "TEST_STATUS=$TEST_STATUS" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          echo "SECURITY_STATUS=$SECURITY_STATUS" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: Post results as comment
        if: always()
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            
            const lintStatus = process.env.LINT_STATUS;
            const testStatus = process.env.TEST_STATUS;
            const securityStatus = process.env.SECURITY_STATUS;
            
            const statusEmoji = (status) => status === 'success' ? '✅' : '❌';
            
            let lintOutput = '';
            let testOutput = '';
            let securityOutput = '';
            
            try {
              lintOutput = fs.readFileSync('lint-output.txt', 'utf8').slice(-2000);
            } catch (e) {}
            
            try {
              testOutput = fs.readFileSync('test-output.txt', 'utf8').slice(-2000);
            } catch (e) {}
            
            try {
              securityOutput = fs.readFileSync('security-output.txt', 'utf8').slice(-2000);
            } catch (e) {}
            
            const comment = `## 🤖 Automated Test Results
            
            **PR:** #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
            **Commit:** ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
            
            ### Results Summary
            
            | Check | Status |
            |-------|--------|
            | Linting | ${statusEmoji(lintStatus)} ${lintStatus} |
            | Tests | ${statusEmoji(testStatus)} ${testStatus} |
            | Security | ${statusEmoji(securityStatus)} ${securityStatus} |
            
            <details>
            <summary>📝 Lint Output</summary>
            
            \`\`\`
            ${lintOutput || 'No output'}
            \`\`\`
            </details>
            
            <details>
            <summary>🧪 Test Output</summary>
            
            \`\`\`
            ${testOutput || 'No output'}
            \`\`\`
            </details>
            
            <details>
            <summary>🔒 Security Scan Output</summary>
            
            \`\`\`
            ${securityOutput || 'No output'}
            \`\`\`
            </details>
            
            ---
            *This is an automated message. Tests were run in a sandboxed environment.*`;
            
            const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
            });
            
            const botComment = comments.find(comment => 
              comment.user.type === 'Bot' && 
              comment.body.includes('🤖 Automated Test Results')
            );
            
            if (botComment) {
              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                comment_id: botComment.id,
                body: comment
              });
            } else {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: context.issue.number,
                body: comment
              });
            }

      - name: Set final status
        if: always()
        run: |
          if [ "${{ steps.test.outcome }}" != "success" ]; then
            echo "Tests failed"
            exit 1
          fi
